revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] to [[Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 塚穴古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = 埴生塚穴古墳 |所属 = [[古市古墳群]] |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきの3丁目 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 33|緯度秒 = 3.58 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 35|経度秒 = 30.55 |ISO = JP-27 |形状 = [[方墳]] |規模 = 一辺54m
高さ10m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]（岩屋山式） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）[[来目皇子]] |陵墓 = 宮内庁治定「埴生崗上墓」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Osaka |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 塚穴古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[来目皇子]]埴生崗上墓 拝所}}]] '''塚穴古墳'''（つかあなこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきのにある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。 [[宮内庁]]により「'''埴生崗上墓'''（はにゅうのおかのえのはか）」として第31代[[用明天皇]][[皇子]]の[[来目皇子]]の墓に治定されている。 == 概要 == 大阪府東部、羽曳野丘陵北端の台地上（標高64メートル）に築造された大型方墳である。一帯には本古墳のほかにもヒチンジョ池西古墳・[[小口山古墳]]などの[[終末期古墳]]が分布する{{Sfn|ふたつの飛鳥の終末期古墳|2010}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の[[来目皇子]]墓として同庁の管理下にあり、[[2008年]]度（[[平成]]20年度）に同庁による墳丘測量調査が、[[2005年]]度（平成17年度）以降に羽曳野市教育委員会による周辺の範囲確認調査などが実施されている。 墳形は方形で、一辺約53-54メートル・高さ約10メートルを測る{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}。墳丘外表施設は明確でないが、墳丘裾部で[[凝灰岩]]による貼石が認められる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第76号|2025}}。墳丘周囲には大規模な掘割と外堤が巡らされており、南側は土塊を用いた上下2段の堅固なつくりで幅16メートル・高さ2.5メートルを、北側は築山のように幅広で幅38メートル・高さ3メートルを測り、外堤を含めた古墳全体としては130メートル四方におよぶ[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html 塚穴古墳]（羽曳野市ホームページ）。。また南外堤には暗渠排水溝が存在する{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口した（現在は埋没）。江戸時代の記録ならびに明治期の記録によれば、[[岩屋山古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]）を標式とする岩屋山式石室とされる。副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|古市遺跡群XXXI|2010}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、前述のように現在は宮内庁により第31代[[用明天皇]]第二皇子の[[来目皇子]]（[[603年]]死去）の墓に治定されている。周辺には本古墳のほかに候補となる大型古墳は存在しないため、来目皇子墓である可能性は高いとされ{{Sfn|ふたつの飛鳥の終末期古墳|2010}}、古墳編年における1つの基準として重要視される古墳になる。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]後期、僧覚峰が石室内部の状況を記録（「埴生崗之古墳」）。 * [[享和]]元年（[[1801年]]）刊行の『河内名所図会』に、覚峰の所見を引用して「塚穴」として記録。 * [[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）、教部省により埴生崗上墓に治定{{Sfn|国史大辞典 埴生崗上墓}}{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}。 * [[1890年]]（明治23年）、拝所などの整備・修営（当時までは石室が開口、「来目皇子御墓御所在見取図」・「来目皇子御墓石槨百分ノ一畧圖」に記録）{{Sfn|国史大辞典 埴生崗上墓}}{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}。 * [[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）、測量図の作成。 * [[2005年|2005]]-[[2006年]]（平成17-18年）、南側の宗教施設建設工事に伴う発掘調査（羽曳野市教育委員会、[[2007年]]に報告）{{Sfn|古市遺跡群XXVIII|2007}}。 * [[2008年]]度（平成20年度）、墳丘測量調査（[[宮内庁書陵部]]、[[2009年]]に報告）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}。 * [[2009年]]度（平成21年度）、羽曳山児童遊園の説明板撤去に伴う範囲確認調査（羽曳野市教育委員会、[[2010年]]に報告）{{Sfn|古市遺跡群XXXI|2010}}。 * [[2023年]]度（[[令和]]5年度）、排水施設工事に伴う事前調査（宮内庁書陵部、[[2025年]]に報告）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第76号|2025}}。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口した（現在は埋没）。石室の規模は次の通り（明治23年作成の石室略図「来目皇子御墓石槨百分ノ一畧圖」による）{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}。 * 玄室：長さ約5.5メートル（18尺）、幅約3.6メートル（12尺）、高さ3メートル以上（10尺余） * 羨道：長さ約7.6メートル（25尺）、幅約1.8メートル（6尺）、高さ2.4メートル以上（8尺余） 江戸時代後期の覚峰の記録と明治23年の石室略図とを比較した際に、玄室の規模に大きな違いはないが、羨道の規模については覚峰は長さを2間半（15尺、4.54メートル）としており、注意が必要となる{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}。 石室の石材は切石とみられる。玄室は2段積みで、奥壁では1石の上に2石、右側壁（西）では2石の上に2石、左側壁（東）では2石の上に3石を積む。羨道は1段積みで、左右とも3石である。天井石は、玄室では1石、羨道では2石。石室内には多量の土砂が流入し、玄室を中心に水が溜まっていたという{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}。 覚峰の記録と石室略図によれば、[[岩屋山古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]）を標式とする岩屋山式石室であることは確実視される{{Sfn|ふたつの飛鳥の終末期古墳|2010}}。近年では、来目皇子の兄の厩戸皇子（[[聖徳太子]]、[[622年]]死去）の墓の[[叡福寺北古墳]]（[[太子町 (大阪府)|太子町]]）も岩屋山式石室であることが確実視されており、岩屋山式石室の年代観を考察するうえで重要視される{{Sfn|ふたつの飛鳥の終末期古墳|2010}}。 江戸時代の記録では、石室内に石棺はないとする。[[大阪狭山市]]の[[狭山池 (大阪府)|狭山池]]では、[[鎌倉時代]]の[[重源]]の改修時に各地の古墳から持ち出されたとみられる多数の石棺が知られるが、塚穴古墳は狭山池に最も近い大型の後期古墳であるため、石室内の石棺は持ち出されてこの狭山池石棺群に含まれる可能性が高いと推測される。特に、大王級の大型の刳抜式家形石棺が使用されたと推測されることから、竜山石製の石棺B（棺蓋）・石棺3（棺身）のセットが第一候補とされる「王者のひつぎ」『王者のひつぎ 狭山池に運ばれた古墳石棺』大阪府立狭山池博物館図録23、大阪府立狭山池博物館、2018年。。 狭山池石棺B.JPG|狭山池石棺B
{{small|[[大阪府立狭山池博物館]]展示。}} 狭山池石棺3.JPG|狭山池石棺3（左端）
{{small|大阪府立狭山池博物館展示。}} == 被葬者 == 塚穴古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第31代[[用明天皇]][[皇子]]の'''[[来目皇子]]'''の墓に治定している。来目皇子（久米王/来目王）は、用明天皇と[[穴穂部間人皇女]]の間の4子のうち、第一皇子の厩戸皇子（[[聖徳太子]]）に次ぐ第二皇子である。『[[日本書紀]]』によれば、[[推古天皇]]10年（[[602年]]）2月に撃新羅将軍となり、諸神部・国造・伴造からなる軍衆2万5千を授けられ、筑紫の嶋郡に赴き船舶を集めて軍糧を運んだが、推古天皇10年6月に病気となり、推古天皇11年（[[603年]]）2月丙子（4日）に筑紫で死去したという。そして「周防娑婆」で殯を行い、[[土師猪手|土師連猪手]]を遣わして殯のことを担当させ、その後に「河内埴生山岡上」に葬ったとする{{Sfn|国史大辞典 来目皇子}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]には、葬所の記載はない。 古来、官道の丹比道が東西に羽曳野丘陵を越える地点（現在の羽曳野市野々上付近）が「埴生坂」と称されており、塚穴古墳の所在地は地名と合致する「埴生坂」『大阪府の地名』日本歴史地名大系28、平凡社、1986年。。[[江戸時代]]後期に至り、駒ヶ谷に所在した金剛輪寺の僧覚峰が、当時「塚穴」と呼ばれた本古墳を来目皇子墓に比定{{Sfn|羽曳野市史 第3巻 史料編1|1994}}。[[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）には教部省により埴生崗上墓に治定され、現在に至っている{{Sfn|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}。周辺には本古墳のほかに候補となる大型古墳は存在しないため、考古学的にも来目皇子墓である可能性は高いとされる{{Sfn|ふたつの飛鳥の終末期古墳|2010}}。 なお、殯を行った「周防娑婆（[[周防国]][[佐波郡]]）」については、[[山口県]][[防府市]]の[[桑山塔ノ尾古墳]]に治定されているが（江戸時代に消滅、出土遺物埋納地が[[宮内庁]]により殯斂地に治定）、考古学的には否定的であり、同市の[[大日古墳]]に比定する説が挙げられる。大日古墳の石室は岩屋山式で、石室内には竜山石製の刳抜式家形石棺を据えており、来目皇子の仮埋葬の棺の可能性が指摘される。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1994|chapter=塚穴古墳|title=羽曳野市史|volume=第3巻 史料編1|publisher=羽曳野市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|羽曳野市史 第3巻 史料編1|1994}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=塚穴古墳|title=古市遺跡群|number=XXVIII|series=羽曳野市埋蔵文化財調査報告書58|publisher=羽曳野市教育委員会|url=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|古市遺跡群XXVIII|2007}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=塚穴古墳|title=古市遺跡群|number=XXXI|series=羽曳野市埋蔵文化財調査報告書64|publisher=羽曳野市教育委員会|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/17333|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|古市遺跡群XXXI|2010}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国文化財総覧」。 * 宮内庁発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2009|chapter=来目皇子 埴生崗上墓の墳丘外形調査報告|title=書陵部紀要|number=60|publisher=宮内庁書陵部|url=https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo060b029.pdf|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 第60号|2009}}}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2025|chapter=来目皇子 埴生崗上墓整備工事予定区域事前調査|title=書陵部紀要 陵墓篇|number=76|publisher=宮内庁書陵部|url=https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo076b063.pdf|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|書陵部紀要 第76号|2025}}}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=北村文治「来目皇子」|ref={{Harvid|国史大辞典 来目皇子}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔「埴生崗上墓」（来目皇子項目内）|ref={{Harvid|国史大辞典 埴生崗上墓}}}}。 * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=塚穴古墳（来目皇子墓）|title=ふたつの飛鳥の終末期古墳 河内飛鳥と大和飛鳥|series=大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館図録50|publisher=[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]|url=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|ふたつの飛鳥の終末期古墳|2010}}}} ==関連項目== * [[来目皇子]] * [[桑山塔ノ尾古墳]]（来目皇子殯斂地） * [[叡福寺北古墳]]（聖徳太子墓） * [[岩屋山古墳]] == 外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html 塚穴古墳] - 羽曳野市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:つかあなこふん はひきのし}} [[Category:古市古墳群]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun (塚穴古墳) | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Habikino, Osaka|Habikino City]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 3.58 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 30.55 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Tsukaana Kofun}} {{citation needed|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Kofun mound | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Habikino, Osaka|Habikino City]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 3.58 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 30.55 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]]

Kofun mound | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Habikino, Osaka|Habikino City]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 3.58 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 30.55 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino, Osaka|qq=}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}} and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yōmei|qq=}}. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayayama Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Province|qq=}} in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * {{ill|Iwayayama Kofun|ja|岩屋山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]]

The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun (塚穴古墳) | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Habikino, Osaka|Habikino City]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 3.58 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 30.55 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]]

10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Kofun of Osaka prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun (塚穴古墳) | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Habikino, Osaka|Habikino City]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 3.58 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 30.55 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture�]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}}

It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}}

Kofun mound | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Habikino, Osaka|Habikino City]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 3.58 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 30.55 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}}

10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}}

Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Imperial tombs of Japan]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun (塚穴古墳) | image = [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun mound | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = [[Habikino, Osaka|Habikino City]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 33 | coordinates = 3.58 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 35 | coordinates = 30.55 | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}}

It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}}
