10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}}

It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11428560]] Wikidata name: Tsukaana Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun (塚穴古墳) | image = File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), funkyu.jpg | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = {{ill|Habikino|qid=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.550994|N|135.591819|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[sn:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}}

10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped stone coffin, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} {{wikidata link|Q11428560}}

10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped Sarcophagus, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} {{wikidata link|Q11428560}}

It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped Sarcophagus, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} {{wikidata link|Q11428560}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]][[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11428560]] Wikidata name: Tsukaana Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun (塚穴古墳) | image = File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), funkyu.jpg | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.550994|N|135.591819|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped Sarcophagus, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} {{wikidata link|Q11428560}}

It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped Sarcophagus, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} {{wikidata link|Q11428560}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. 10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped Sarcophagus, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} {{wikidata link|Q11428560}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11428560}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped Sarcophagus, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} {{wikidata link|Q11428560}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11428560}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]][[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11428560]] Wikidata name: Tsukaana Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun (塚穴古墳) | image = File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), funkyu.jpg | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.550994|N|135.591819|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped Sarcophagus, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} {{wikidata link|Q11428560}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11428560}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped Sarcophagus, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11428560}}

Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11428560}}

[[commons:Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]][[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)]][[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11428560]] Wikidata name: Tsukaana Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tsukaana Kofun (塚穴古墳) | image = File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), funkyu.jpg | alternate_name = Hanyū Tsukaana Kofun | part_of = [[Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] | location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.550994|N|135.591819|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = 54m on each side
10m in height | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (Iwayayama style) | burial goods_found = | Associated_tombs = | built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] | Interred_individual = Determined by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as [[Prince Kume|Prince Kume]] | Historic Site = "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" by the Imperial Household Agency | Historic_sites = | Tangible_cultural_properties = | Special_notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = 方墳 | map_dot_label = Tsukaana Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[Prince Kume]]'s Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka (Imperial burial)}}]] '''Tsukaana Kofun''' (塚穴古墳) is a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is classified as a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the "Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka" (埴生崗上墓), the burial site of {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Overview == Located on a plateau at the northern edge of the Habikino Hills (elevation 64 meters) in eastern Osaka Prefecture, the Tsukaana Kofun is a large square tumulus, measuring approximately 53–54 meters on each side and 10 meters in height. It is part of a group of late-period kofun, including the {{ill|Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja|ヒチンジョ池西古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hichinjoike-nishi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Oguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is currently under the management of the Imperial Household Agency, which conducted a survey of the tumulus in 2008. The surrounding area has been surveyed by the {{ill|Habikino City Board of Education|ja|羽曳野市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} since 2005. The mound consists of three tiers. Though no surface features like stone coverings remain visible, traces of tuff stone suggest such features once existed. Surrounding the mound is a wide moat and embankment, measuring 130 meters square in total, with the southern embankment structured in two levels and the northern embankment broader, at 38 meters wide and 3 meters high. The burial chamber, now buried, is a two-tiered yokoana-style stone chamber that opened to the south. Historical records from the Edo period and Meiji era describe its design as consistent with the Iwayayama style seen in [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]]. == History == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: The monk Kakuhō documented the interior of the stone chamber. * 1801: Listed as "Tsukaana" in the {{ill|Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja|河内名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 1875: Designated as Hanyū Kōjō no Hakaka by the Ministry of Education. * 1890: Restoration of the tomb area, during which the stone chamber was sealed. * 1925: A survey of the site was conducted. * 2005–2006: Excavations carried out due to nearby construction. * 2008: Survey by the Imperial Household Agency. == Burial Chamber == The burial chamber is a two-tiered yokoana-style chamber, likely constructed of hewn stone. Measurements (based on Edo period and Meiji records): * Burial chamber: Approx. 5.5m in length, 3.6m in width, 3m in height. * Entrance corridor: Approx. 7.6m in length, 1.8m in width, 2.4m in height. It is thought that the chamber once contained a large house-shaped Sarcophagus, fragments of which might have been removed during the Kamakura period. == Interred_individual == The Imperial Household Agency attributes the tomb to {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}}, son of Emperor Yōmei and Anahobe no Hashihito. Historical records describe his death in [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 603, with burial later conducted at Hanyū Kōjō. == See Also == * {{ill|Prince Kume|ja|来目皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kume|qq=}} * {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}} (Temporary burial site of Prince Kume) * [[:en:Iwayayama Kofun|Iwayayama Kofun]] == External Links == * [https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/kofun_shumatsuki/2398.html Tsukaana Kofun] – Habikino City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|version=104342288|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] }} [[ja:塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)]] [[ja:塚穴古墳]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11428560}}
